speaker: MATT
raw_text: I certainly hope not. Also, next cool announcement: our first Critical Role comic came out today. Our four glorious first panels starting off the past adventure of the party in Westruun for their first Winter's Crest Festival. So yeah, there will be one four-panel comic coming out each week to go through the six week run of this storyline, written by our fantastic Taliesin Jaffe and Marisha Ray over here, and drawn by the incredible--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Go log on to GeekandSundry.com, slash "find it on that site somewhere."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's on the front page right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And you can find it on Spotify. There's some cool songs on there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's baby-making music right there, baby.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. There's some panty peelers in there, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh god.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's a terrible term.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I wrote it in the song description. Somebody edited it out. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Well.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go check that out, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Go check that out, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Exactly. It's actually a pretty great playlist; I've been listening to it all afternoon while prepping for this. By the way, it's really interesting to write stuff for the current scenario in Whitestone with some Marvin Gaye in the background.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Are we going to feel that influence in tonight's game?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Or just 24 hours of a close-up of a turkey. A turkey I cooked, so undercooked. But yeah, so we won't be here next Thursday, but we'll be thinking of you lovingly in the process, and we'll be returning the Thursday after that to continue the story.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Solid.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Let's see, a couple other announcements. We will be off next week for Thanksgiving because all of us have families to see, so there will be no Critical Role next week, unfortunately. I think we'll be doing-- are we doing a rebroadcast of something?

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: We'll probably do a rebroadcast of all the episodes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Like a marathon?

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Sure, why not.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No. I'm going to one day do a Gilmore mini in private. It'll be fun. All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Switched over to PayPal. While he looks at that, one last thing. We are, as always, friends with Wyrmwood Gaming. If you want beautiful wooden boxes in all kinds of colors and patterns, go to Wyrmwood Gaming, and if you use the code CritRole, you will get free shipping.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Say it again. CritRole.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You'll get free shipping. Hey, one nerd helping out another.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: They are so sexy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They are very sexy. And they have magnets in them to keep your dice protected when they're in your other bags. Your Bag of Holding.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Christmas is just around the corner.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It keeps my Critmas bronze? Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah, you've got your little mini.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know I'm so excited I have a mini! I'm not even in the game, really, but I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's Gilmore's mini. Any time Gilmore talks, can he have that mini?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Anyway! Let's get kicking into this evening's episode of Critical Role. So. To get all of you up to speed. The party has traveled to Whitestone, partially to free their name of some negative political ramifications back in their home city of Emon, and to aid Percy in possibly freeing his hometown of Whitestone from the oppression of Lord and Lady Briarwood, a couple who have risen to power over the past five years after slaughtering his family and taking it over and keeping it all this time. After arriving, they found a very heavy undead presence in the town, with giant zombies roaming the streets as sentinels. Most of the townsfolk kept under perpetual watch in this slowly will-crushing atmosphere. But as Vox Machina arrived, they began to instill the thoughts of rebellion. Stoking those flames once again with the return of the surviving de Rolo, they began to sway the people's minds to make a final stand. During that time, they also discovered that Percy's sister Cassandra is also a surviving de Rolo and exists somewhere in Castle Whitestone, seemingly under lock and key. The party began to systematically take out members of the new nobles who worked underneath the Briarwoods, setting many buildings on fire, leaving emblems of the de Rolo crest across the town and written in the sky, and after an extensive battle, began to make their way toward Castle Whitestone itself. Burrowing into a partially collapsed but long-remembered secret passage underneath the castle, the party rested for the evening with the help of their recently arrived cleric Pike, who had managed to project herself through the will of Sarenrae as a spiritual body given temporary physical form. While they were searching this tunnel, it led to the dungeons, the prison cells beneath the Castle Whitestone where Percy once woke up five years before, before Cassandra helped him escape. While they were walking through those cells, they discovered an old woman currently locked down below there who is asking for the party's aid in freeing her from her imprisonment. And last time we left off, you were helping with--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Christmas is just around the corner.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It keeps my Critmas bronze? Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah, you've got your little mini.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know I'm so excited I have a mini! I'm not even in the game, really, but I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's Gilmore's mini. Any time Gilmore talks, can he have that mini?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. I'm going to one day do a Gilmore mini in private. It'll be fun. All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I brought this for good luck today. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's going to be my woobie.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Hey, now. Not cool. Not cool. We do not use the W word around here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Does he even know what he's saying?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he knows.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're off the rails again.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fucking vampires.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Did you use woobie intentionally?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Goddamn it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's in Mr. Mom!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: They don't know where you're pointing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Point down!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's in Mr. Mom. This guy right here.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Anyway! Let's get kicking into this evening's episode of Critical Role. So. To get all of you up to speed. The party has traveled to Whitestone, partially to free their name of some negative political ramifications back in their home city of Emon, and to aid Percy in possibly freeing his hometown of Whitestone from the oppression of Lord and Lady Briarwood, a couple who have risen to power over the past five years after slaughtering his family and taking it over and keeping it all this time. After arriving, they found a very heavy undead presence in the town, with giant zombies roaming the streets as sentinels. Most of the townsfolk kept under perpetual watch in this slowly will-crushing atmosphere. But as Vox Machina arrived, they began to instill the thoughts of rebellion. Stoking those flames once again with the return of the surviving de Rolo, they began to sway the people's minds to make a final stand. During that time, they also discovered that Percy's sister Cassandra is also a surviving de Rolo and exists somewhere in Castle Whitestone, seemingly under lock and key. The party began to systematically take out members of the new nobles who worked underneath the Briarwoods, setting many buildings on fire, leaving emblems of the de Rolo crest across the town and written in the sky, and after an extensive battle, began to make their way toward Castle Whitestone itself. Burrowing into a partially collapsed but long-remembered secret passage underneath the castle, the party rested for the evening with the help of their recently arrived cleric Pike, who had managed to project herself through the will of Sarenrae as a spiritual body given temporary physical form. While they were searching this tunnel, it led to the dungeons, the prison cells beneath the Castle Whitestone where Percy once woke up five years before, before Cassandra helped him escape. While they were walking through those cells, they discovered an old woman currently locked down below there who is asking for the party's aid in freeing her from her imprisonment. And last time we left off, you were helping with--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Christmas is just around the corner.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It keeps my Critmas bronze? Thank you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah, you've got your little mini.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I know I'm so excited I have a mini! I'm not even in the game, really, but I am.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. I'm going to one day do a Gilmore mini in private. It'll be fun. All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I brought this for good luck today. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's going to be my woobie.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Hey, now. Not cool. Not cool. We do not use the W word around here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Does he even know what he's saying?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're off the rails again.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fucking vampires.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Did you use woobie intentionally?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Goddamn it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's in Mr. Mom!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: They don't know where you're pointing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Point down!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's in Mr. Mom. This guy right here.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: She leans forward and stands up and steps into the light, and you have a better look of her now, and her face is a little weathered from travel. You can see she definitely doesn't appear to be nobleborn, at least. She's by no means a homely looking individual, but you can see she's seen the world, or elements of it, to have those dark lines at the sides of her face, whether it be from smiles or frowning. She steps into the light and looks to you, this peasant individual making these threats, and seems partially shaken but keeping her composure in the face of such threats. She says, "Well, for one thing, I've been here with them for the past five years. I helped them reestablish their current social control of this city when they left it a pile of flames and rubble. And their little experiment beneath the castle? I was one of their chief scientific designers."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll an intimidation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's pretty good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 16, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And just as he finishes talking, I throw a dagger a foot away from her head in the wall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You gave her a fucking dagger? And then it flashes back to you, right? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So the dagger which is thrown (whoosh) hits the stone wall next to her and jams itself between two of the stone bricks and gets wedged there for a second. She looks over for a second, reaches up for it and grabs the hilt as it vanishes in a puff of smoke.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You are so fucking lucky it did that. (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I'm down here because my work is done and I wish not to linger with an impending battle. I don't choose sides; I merely let them fight while I live another day far, far away. I wouldn't have gotten this far dealing with the people I've dealt with in the past if I had chosen to stick around under such circumstances, so I tried to leave. It appears Anders saw me packing and decided to gain 'a little favor by turning me in, so they threw me down here, told me to wait out the rest of the experiment, at least until the ziggurat was complete. So here I am, living amongst rats in a cell for over a week. That's my reward."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I see if she's lying?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can. Make an insight check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not great.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's a number.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It is a number. 16.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 16? Okay. You get the sense that she's telling the truth. You're not quite sure how far off the truth, but there doesn't seem to be any sort of bald-faced deception in her voice, at this point. She speaks with a confidence and a brusqueness of a person who is frustrated by the circumstance and has no other choice. "I wasn't interested in their zealotry or the whispered dealings, just the challenge behind the construction. I enjoy challenge. Challenge that pays, well, (chuckles) I enjoy that even more."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Question two  What are you doing down here, really?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About a minute and a half of awkward quiet as the sound of scraping metal and jimmying thieves' tools, eventually (click).

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: She's a nervous laugher. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and make a check lock pick. This will be more difficult than normal because--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I snapped off a thing in there. Sorry about that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, I'm going to use Luck.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You're going to use Luck now?!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Shall I inspire you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Don't use Luck yet

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's not that bad. That's a 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's a 20 baseline?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, because of my--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Expertise? All right. 20 is exactly what you needed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Skadoosh.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That was the next one. So you take a moment you walk up all slick and (click).

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait. One sec. This never happens. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have to get down and you spend the next 30 seconds actually managing to get the pin that she has stuck in there out without actually breaking the spring inside.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry about that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Fucking doors!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Shut up!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It never ends!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Push it open (creak) The door opens. Everyone before you whips around, hearing the noise in the silence. Looking inside, it appears to be a long-untouched music room, a space for hosting individuals where you would have small performances, and there is a very cold fireplace in the far corner. There is a small, circular window that is placed in the center of the room that has a very thick glass pane with cross bar on it that seems to look out. And this is your first view-- for you, especially, since you woke up in the tunnel-- your first view of the outside.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: As we're walking, I just want to very gently take my gun--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And shoot her with it. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And just put it gently at her back, going, nothing personal. I'm just feeling a bit nervous.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Understood." She straightens up a little more and continues walking ever so carefully. Not quite leaning her head or making too many large movements, but definitely still keeping a seemingly nervous eye on how quiet the atmosphere is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I creep over to one of the doors in the room and see if I can hear anything on the other side.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You don't hear anything inside the room. Roll a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (groans) 14.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear some slight, muffled, dull thud noise, but you can't really tell how close it is. It's very, very faint.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I see if the door handle is--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (click) It's open.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I step to the side and push it open.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, it dries and gets rubbed off. It lasts about an hour after application.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't need it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, you do now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Has it worn off?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It lasts for a minute, so yeah, it's worn off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, it only lasts for a minute?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Friends only lasts for a minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I had enhance charisma on for a while, but now it's gone, so if you do it, what I had is gone--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Pour some sugar on, Keyleth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll do it. I'm going to enhance. In case we get busted. So you'll have advantage on persuasion rolls.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you have advantage on all charisma-based ability checks.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right, putting that down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Also, no one forget that we have Heroes' Feast.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wait, if I put the holy oil on things, it lasts for a few hours, right? It lasts for half a day or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'd say it lasts for about an hour after application.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Wait until we're in the thick of it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The oil will eventually just drift off, as it is an oil.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It dries and stuff.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. So both will attune to you at your next short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Percy!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Even if he's there, if she's not there, we'll leave.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Go. Lead the way.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're running out of time.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Brother, run and check the study.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I start running towards the direction that was described.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go towards the under--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Very well.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Lead the way down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No talking.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, we're going down?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we're not leaving Vax.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're heading down. He's going to catch up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She begins making her way towards the landing that leads to the stairway down, whereas you stealth off in that direction. You find your way around that corner. Go ahead and roll perception.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: As I'm moving, by the way, it's minor, but I take out Clarota's (sniffs, groans) metallic cap and put it on, and I also whip out this other cloak that I've been looking at and whip it around. You're not here. And now continue on.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. How many charges were restored in the wand from your last long rest?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I continue my long journey into the room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You just get past there, and you can see into the room. You have visual on, yeah, pretty much everything in the room from there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So I have to make a ranged shot if I'm to do anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What do I see? Can I see how rough Vax is?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At a glance, it's hard to tell.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm bleeding out my mouth.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's also facing away from you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Looks normal to me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You see Grog and Vax facing off the guy in the corner. You see two of these large armored entities.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'll go for them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wand of Magic Missiles. And I'll shoot three at the one at the left. That's the first charge, and then use three additional charges on the one on the right.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, that's his turn. That was his action. He's going to use his bonus action to say, "Come to my aid! Quickly!" He's going to put another inspiration die to the other armor. And that's going to end his turn. It's now their turn. They are both going to come around. This one is just going to move around Grog; no attack of opportunity because it's still in your melee. This one is going to step into range from over here. This one is going to turn and go for Percy. Sees this billowing as you're stuck there trying to fix the gun, and just rushing towards you with its shield forward, its horrible, jagged longsword in the air arcing towards you. Two strikes versus you. That'll be a 19?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Oh lord bards. Shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, I have to tell a level, don't I?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you can tell me what level.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do I know, because he's a bard, what he's doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You are very familiar with the spell. It is a 4th-level spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Then I cast it at fourth level.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Then there is no roll. With that, he takes a moment to finish casting. You see there's a moment where this bit of purple arcane energy, familiar to most of you as Scanlan's escapes, begins to sparkle behind him and as he goes to step into it, the shawm shrieks out into the room, he (groans) reaches up and covers his eye as it almost seems to be shrill to his head and cause the pain to pulse and the doorway just slams behind him. (panting)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Uh-uh, that door is locked, motherfucker! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Get used to it, because I am! (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "You've been fighting them for what, a few days? I've been fighting them for nearly three years. You have no power over me, brother. And if our goals are aligned to free this city, let's do it together. But I don't have to listen to you."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're putting an end to the Briarwoods tonight. Before the sun sets. This is the end of them. It's all going to be over, and we're going to stop whatever it is they're doing under the city.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You say this. We've tried twice."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I know. This is it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's what the others thought. You left me, you know."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I know, and I'm so sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Good. Well, if we're going to do this, let's get ready."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're going to your room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, I am."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: To wait until this is over.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No. No, I'm not. I'm going to my room to get my things. To get my mother's armor."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (sputters) You're-- I don't think that's a very good idea.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, she goes off to her room. You're there for a few minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I whistle at Trinket to get him to stop.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, as you get to the landing you whistle out, and Trinket is just now pushing through the door and gets out for a bit, looking back and forth. Looks over the shoulder, and runs back to you. It appears that Ripley has scattered into Whitestone somewhere.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Damn it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We stop.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We need to find the Briarwoods.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm still upstairs. Are you going to come meet us again upstairs?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We'll get Trinket and go back upstairs, wherever they are.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Taking a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We're taking a short rest?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're waiting there. They followed her down; I'm waiting on Cassandra.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I could stay down in that foyer.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Come up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Are you waiting in the study?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, waiting in the study.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, if you're taking a short rest, you can take the short rest to inspect the boots, get the feel for them, they are indeed enchanted. They are a pair of Boots of Levitation. Once per day, you can trigger the levitation for up to ten minutes as per the spell. You can't actually move as part of it, you just levitate in the air, and you have to push off to get space.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We don't want him coming back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's crumpled up against the desk in the corner, there's all these papers that got pushed off the side, there were a bunch of random scrawlings.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 24? Okay. You search his body. You find 120 gold pieces on him. Not a whole lot. He doesn't keep his important shit on him, for the most part. You do find, however, the boots that he's wearing are very finely crafted, and unlike the rest of his wear, have a hand-stitching in it that is exemplary in construction and doesn't match with the rest of his ensemble. So it seems like something you'd wear more for function than for actual style. Pardon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I take them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You take them.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Take his shoes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Does anybody have magic? Can you see what these are?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Sure, I can look at them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Look at these, Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I look at them.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You glance through and inspect them, and you take the next ten minutes or so to get the gist of these letters while Cassandra's away. Many of them talk about a slew of things that are disconcerting to you as the information hits you, and you relay this information to the group. Notes on the removal of key magical defenses during the political meeting at Emon. The discussion, as in making sure that certain individuals and safeguards that are usually made during political meetings like that were not present during that endeavor.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, wait. Give me that. I take it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You have a poison-tipped bolt.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For a tiny little hand-crossbow is essentially what it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: So now I've got to unlock it, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Hey, 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, not a natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay. Easy enough to unlock at this point, you get it open, you pull it open, there's a collection of letters.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Percival, come here. You're probably the best person to know what to make of these.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A manse is a place or a home; it has many different meanings. They're warning Anders, and there are multiple letters at different periods of time over the past few years that are warning Anders of an arrival of Seeker Asum's spies.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Percival, come here. You're probably the best person to know what to make of these.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You glance through and inspect them, and you take the next ten minutes or so to get the gist of these letters while Cassandra's away. Many of them talk about a slew of things that are disconcerting to you as the information hits you, and you relay this information to the group. Notes on the removal of key magical defenses during the political meeting at Emon. The discussion, as in making sure that certain individuals and safeguards that are usually made during political meetings like that were not present during that endeavor.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Evidence is what that is!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Notes on best construction methods using the mined whitestone and repairs for the manse in the ziggurat.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: The manse?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As in a mansion?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 30 days. Glancing within the kitchen, there doesn't appear to be much that catches your attention.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is in the same handwriting.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And is it signed?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It is signed a Lord Riskel Daxio.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What! Who? Axio?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Riskel Daxio.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Riskel Daxio.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We know him!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Who is he?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's on the Council of Tal'Dorei.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps) Oh, motherfucker! He's a spy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's our fucking spy!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He's technically also the one that helped designate the construction of your keep and plotted the construction stuff--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh no.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He built our keep!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's the construction guy, he's like our head builder.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He could be spying on us around the keep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He could have a secret entrance to our Keep that he built and didn't tell us.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Holy moly.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As soon as I see that, I go down in a crouch and I shit-scry on our keep to see if it's still there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, that's right, you did that! And I put an elemental there. It's babysitting.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, you did.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hopefully still there. 30 days.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: She's currently now dressed in armor you've seen as ornamental before. It was armor that your mother trained in when she was young, but was unused for a long time. It seems to be serviceable; it's been updated and such. She slides a shortsword into a sheath at her side, and her hair is pulled back and tied off, and she looks at the rest of the room. "So are we ready?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Bacon?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, bacon. Did you not know that's what the word was?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: So everything's fine at the keep?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At the time.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: At the time, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At least, there's nothing on fire, there's no screams, there's no movement. It just seems like the cold kitchen is untouched.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Guys, my shit saw no movement. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Good to know, Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You've squeezed all you're going to be able to get out of that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Percy. Can we get into the underground caverns from here?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Can I think of a way? I'm assuming I know a way to where this might be.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you have this conversation, a voice from the doorway rings out. "I can take you." You look over, and it's Cassandra.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: She's going to take us.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is that your sister?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Apparently.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "After the arrows felled me, I recovered. Father Reynal found me. Brought me from the brink at the temple of Pelor outside of town. Things hadn't fallen quite that far back then. We had more people in the city. At the time, the Briarwood rule was fresh and folks were eager to speak out against what they had wrought on this city, the fall of the de Rolos. We still thought they had fallen to plague, but through some unfortunate political business, they had come to power, but we began to question it. I knew better. I told the truth once I'd recovered. We began to build our first rebellion. Two years we built up the resistance before we made our first move. They seemed to know exactly what we were plotting. Cut us off at every angle. Couldn't see who it was who was giving up our information, but I knew it was close. But before I had the opportunity, I was helming a group that was traveling to utilize the escape route that I took you through when they first attacked. It was just after dusk, and Lord Briarwood was there waiting for us in the forest. He slaughtered most of the men, but he recognized my face from the dinner we had the night they took the castle. They did not harm me, strangely. They treated me like a guest against my will, in my own house. I seemed to ascertain that by keeping me there, having a member of the house within their grasp, they could use that as leverage. I don't know. Sylas, he had his charms, and there were many that worked beneath them against their own will. It did not work on me. My hatred was too strong. So instead, they tried to provide me with wealth. Power, here in Whitestone. I just took to reading and ignoring it all. I don't know why they kept me around, but I wasn't going to question it if it gave me a chance to possibly undermine them." She grabs the side of her sword as you guys have now reached now down the long hallway towards the back of the castle. You know the direction she's taking you. It leads to the cellar, to the undercroft and the de Rolo mausoleum. "Three years of reading in that library. Father's library. Strategy. Tactics. Old lines of information, as well as the myths and history of Whitestone. I found allies within the castle. I contacted Yennen, and through him, Archibald, who helped me build the first rebellion. Archibald still lives, Percy. He's been completely necessary for what we've built here." She looks down at her armor as you guys reach the edge of the cellar, and she pulls the door open quietly. There's wooden stairs that lead down into the cellar. She taps her armor. "This was Mother's. It's good to know I'm not the only tomboy in the bloodline." Brushes the hair out of her face, and for the first time really looks at you with a smile since she first saw you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And I walk over to Cassandra. I don't give her the gun, I just say, you're Cassandra?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I was at the door. He had a knife to your throat. He wanted you to say something to us. What did he want you to say?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "He wanted me to shout for help. He wanted me to draw you in."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I insight check her to see if she's telling the truth?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You've been living here. How did it come that he had a blade to your throat today? How did it all unravel? Have you been living here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 24. She's telling the truth.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, after the arrows felled me-- I guess I should start at the beginning." And she's walking along with you guys as she's leading you down the stairs and down to the bottom floor. She doesn't look to you. She's still looking ahead, very intent.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I take that pistol back from you.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "After the arrows felled me, I recovered. Father Reynal found me. Brought me from the brink at the temple of Pelor outside of town. Things hadn't fallen quite that far back then. We had more people in the city. At the time, the Briarwood rule was fresh and folks were eager to speak out against what they had wrought on this city, the fall of the de Rolos. We still thought they had fallen to plague, but through some unfortunate political business, they had come to power, but we began to question it. I knew better. I told the truth once I'd recovered. We began to build our first rebellion. Two years we built up the resistance before we made our first move. They seemed to know exactly what we were plotting. Cut us off at every angle. Couldn't see who it was who was giving up our information, but I knew it was close. But before I had the opportunity, I was helming a group that was traveling to utilize the escape route that I took you through when they first attacked. It was just after dusk, and Lord Briarwood was there waiting for us in the forest. He slaughtered most of the men, but he recognized my face from the dinner we had the night they took the castle. They did not harm me, strangely. They treated me like a guest against my will, in my own house. I seemed to ascertain that by keeping me there, having a member of the house within their grasp, they could use that as leverage. I don't know. Sylas, he had his charms, and there were many that worked beneath them against their own will. It did not work on me. My hatred was too strong. So instead, they tried to provide me with wealth. Old lines of information, as well as the myths and history of Whitestone. I found allies within the castle. I contacted Yennen, and through him, Archibald, who helped me build the first rebellion. Brushes the hair out of her face, and for the first time really looks at you with a smile since she first saw you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And I walk over to Cassandra. I don't give her the gun, I just say, you're Cassandra?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I was at the door. He had a knife to your throat. He wanted you to say something to us. What did he want you to say?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "He wanted me to shout for help. He wanted me to draw you in."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can I insight check her to see if she's telling the truth?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You've been living here. How did it come that he had a blade to your throat today? How did it all unravel? Have you been living here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 24. She's telling the truth.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, after the arrows felled me-- I guess I should start at the beginning." And she's walking along with you guys as she's leading you down the stairs and down to the bottom floor. She doesn't look to you. She's still looking ahead, very intent.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I take that pistol back from you.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Grog, you immediately see a cluster of recent footprints, probably a day old at most, that lead in towards the tunnel. Looks like the prints are of seven individuals. Feet are fairly wide, masculine and heavy in step. One gait appears to be smaller, thinner, and more feminine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: How many of us found this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, two of you saw it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I've got one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you've got one piece.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And Percy's got the other?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Second piece, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You want to burn one?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I walk away from the group all the way to the back, and I take it--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: To where you came from?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, towards where we came from. What's behind us? Is it just tunnel back that way?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You mean on that path you're going?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I push through the back of the group to the back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: What's behind us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Where you just came from is the stairs that lead up to the castle.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I was going to throw it, but...

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I see Vex looking around. Can I look for tracks as well? I'm proficient in survival, and I never use it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Natural 20! I swear to god.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: While you sit there inspecting the dirt--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It looks like there's some tracks here, but I don't know what it's saying.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: There's one round that you three get to act. The rest of you have a surprise round right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Grog, you immediately see a cluster of recent footprints, probably a day old at most, that lead in towards the tunnel. Looks like the prints are of seven individuals. Feet are fairly wide, masculine and heavy in step. One gait appears to be smaller, thinner, and more feminine.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How many tombs are open around us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Eight total in this room.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Should we check the tombs?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They're already open and empty.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They're open and empty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You all make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All of us?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, 20 and above that'd be just you two?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah, three. All right, so you three, you notice the temperature drop ever so slightly in the room and in the darkened shadow. you look around and you see what appears to be some sort of ethereal mass drifting away.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Damn it!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: God damn it! Here we go again.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We've got company.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay, that ends your turn. It's now their go. This one drifts forward to Keyleth. This one drifts forward to Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We need magic! I'm taking my last shot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That was seven, by the way. Seven 20s this game.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's stupid.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, see? That's better. 15.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 15 does hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All right. They're easy to hit, at least. That's such a shitty roll. 12 points of ice damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. The ice shot (gunshot) tears through it, doing additional damage. You see part of its form crystallize from the little bit of ectoplasm that's floating in the air, but it's not enough to actually disperse its form. It continues pushing forward. You would have to reload to use those again.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm out.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's now their turn. This one currently loses interest in you and drifts two, three, four, five, six, seven, eight-- it (whoosh) passes through the stone. Moves through and appears on the other side. I need you to make a charisma saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The first one didn't hit, so you only get the 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, just the 20!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Just 20, so as the first one misses, you pull back, trying to stop him, but the hammer still slams you in the side; you don't have time to move out of the way, and it pushes you up against the wall. You land on your feet, and you can feel the pulsing of the blood broken beneath the skin. That ends Grog's go. Percy, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (gasps, sighs) "Hi!" I saw what just happened, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to try to fix my gun.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That fixes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (sighs) Are you better? Good, then let's deal with this. I'm going to start diving around the columns to get away from the wraith.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Around here?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, because I don't have a shot this round.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you move around that way.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. That ends your turn?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That ends my turn.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Keyleth falls unconscious to the ground as this blast of radiant energy bursts around her as this spiritual radiant form that's been surrounding the area and stalking the various individuals. It slams into her form. As she collapses, the ghost is jettisoned from her body and looks like it's been taking the radiant damage and is having a hard time keeping its corporeal form. All right, that ends Keyleth's turn. Keyleth, I need you to roll a death saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And as a bonus action, I'll do a Healing Word on Vex. Right? You're injured badly?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, not badly, but--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, well then fuck it. Oh, you're injured badly? Would I know that?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You could glance over and look at him as he pulls up to you, and see him (pants).

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I've got gunshots in my back.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Yeah, so I'll Healing Word.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll the healing points for that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What is it? Shit. It's right here  d4 plus five. That's it?! So lame. Seven points.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. That ends your turn. Keyleth, at the top of your turn, roll 3d8 radiant damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, yes. Oh, this is going to knock you out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hopefully.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That's nine already.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm down.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Six more is 15.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's now Cass's turn. Cass, who's having a weird time with the whole process of seeing a bunch of herselves and her brothers stabbing each other and getting possessed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 25. As you pull back the arrow and you see the flames ignite around the shaft, you release it. It slams into the ghost, and you see its form (raspy cry) disperse from the impact, the arrow hitting the wall behind it and shattering against the stone. Ghost destroyed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: For my second attack, I'm going to attack Percy. Can I see him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep, right over there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: All right, I'm going to do it. 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 21 hits.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (ghost noises)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 16 points of damage to you, Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yep.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That was also through my Blazing Bowstring.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you're using both Blazing Bowstring abilities right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. A flaming arrow (impact noise) sticks into the shoulder, and you can feel the flames burning into your body--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Plus another five. That was another five burning damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There you go.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm still doing fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I hate these things.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: At the end of the short rest, everyone gains an additional 1d8. It's nice. Doesn't hurt.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're going to say it, aren't you?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I double back to Keyleth and start trying to administer some sort of aid.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hi, I'm dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Medicine check? Is that it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can make a medicine check to try to stabilize her, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 18, yeah. You stabilize the bleeding. She's no longer making death saving throws. She is still unconscious. You all reconvene. Take a breath.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, is Keyleth alive?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh Jesus! Keyleth, I'm sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm stabilized.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: She needs help. Scanlan, come here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's pull ourselves together.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I will sing a song of rest, which will give us 1d8 for everybody.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For hit dice spent, or just for the rest?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. It just says 1d8. It's my song of rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Song of rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I've got a lot of health potions.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I've got three.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Would you like one?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Probably.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What if we take a short rest?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys take a short rest here to roll some hit dice.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Wait, is Keyleth alive?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh Jesus! Keyleth, I'm sorry.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm stabilized.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: She needs help. Scanlan, come here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's pull ourselves together.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I will sing a song of rest, which will give us 1d8 for everybody.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For hit dice spent, or just for the rest?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. It just says 1d8. It's my song of rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Song of rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I've got a lot of health potions.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I've got three.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Would you like one?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Probably.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What if we take a short rest?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At the end of the short rest, everyone gains an additional 1d8. It's nice. Doesn't hurt.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I come to consciousness by Scanlan's singing?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) You're down with more HP. Yeah, you know me!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hit dice?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we can roll some hit dice.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: For the next episode. See you guys in two weeks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Understandably so.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Creepiest character in the Marvel universe.

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: How do we do this?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: He is the creepiest creep.

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Whoever plays him has to know you're going to get your ass kicked.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's Tennant, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's Tennant. That's also who I dressed up as at the superhero party. I painted myself purple.

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: You did Purple Man? Why would you do that?!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There's an amazing photo of a friend of ours dressed as Luke Cage, just beating the shit out of me. It's amazing.

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Well, that's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He also sang Gogol Bordello's--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: "Start Wearing Purple."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It was good.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It was creepy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'm just excited to see all the Doctor Who Tennant fans watch the series for Tennant and then be like, "Oh no, no, no!"

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Sorry, guys. No bueno.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's just going to be bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: ZAC
preceding_turns.text: Sweet. That's it for tonight, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Thank you guys so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: See you in two weeks!
